title: QoreChain Implements Complete NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Stack on Public Mainnet - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Swiss-registered blockchain QoreChain executed a live mainnet transaction using a complete end-to-end NIST post-quantum cryptography stack (ML-DSA-87, ML-KEM-1024, SHAKE-256) applied across the entire protocol path, positioning it ahead of peers that only did partial edge-level quantum-resistant signatures.

insights.claim: QoreChain recorded a live public mainnet transaction (1,000 QOR on July 2, 2026) using a complete end-to-end post-quantum cryptography stack covering signatures, key exchanges, and hash functions simultaneously, rather than partial transaction-edge signatures.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: First full-protocol-path PQC blockchain deployment sets a competitive bar; monitor whether rival L1s move beyond partial edge implementations.
insights.quote: this network deployment marks a milestone by applying standardized post-quantum math across the protocol’s entire cryptographic path—covering signatures, key exchanges, and hash functions simultaneously

insights.claim: QoreChain's PQC stack uses ML-DSA-87 (FIPS 204 / Dilithium-5, Category 5) for signatures, ML-KEM-1024 (FIPS 203 / Kyber) for key encapsulation, and SHAKE-256 for hashing, integrating NIST FIPS 203/204 algorithms natively in the genesis state layer rather than as application-level patches.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Native genesis-layer PQC is harder to retrofit and signals durable design; a template for other chains facing crypto-agility mandates.

insights.claim: The deployment explicitly targets the 'Harvest Now, Decrypt Later' vulnerability, where actors record encrypted block data and public key histories from ledgers today to compromise them retroactively once fault-tolerant quantum computers exist.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Blockchains with permanent public ledgers are uniquely exposed to retroactive decryption; PQC readiness becomes a differentiating security claim for capital.
insights.quote: The comprehensive layer-1 deployment directly targets the “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later” vulnerability

insights.claim: QoreChain pairs hybrid secp256k1 + ML-DSA-87 signatures with an AI-optimized PRISM Consensus engine running deterministic on-chain reinforcement learning to dynamically tune node parameters, and runs a triple-VM runtime (EVM, CosmWasm, SVM) with native cross-VM messaging on a single chain.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Hybrid classical+PQC signatures plus multi-VM support target broad web3 developer capture; watch adoption vs. established L1s.
insights.locator: Rather than applying temporary application-level patches

insights.claim: QoreChain operates under the Swiss DLT Act via the QoreChain Association in Rolle, Switzerland, holds a FINMA no-action letter classifying QOR as a utility token, and runs a deflationary model where 30% of all gas fees are permanently burned.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Regulatory clarity plus fee-burn tokenomics matter for token investors weighing supply dynamics and legal risk.
insights.locator: Operated under the Swiss DLT Act
